content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of length NNN such that 1≤Ai≤N1 \leq A_i \leq N1≤Ai​≤N and Ai≠i,A_i \neq i,Ai​=i, ∀i∈[1,N]\forall i \in [1, N]∀i∈[1,N].

Count the number of arrays BBB of length NNN such that ∀i∈[1,N]\forall i \in [1, N]∀i∈[1,N]:


Bi≠BAiB_i \neq B_{A_i}Bi​=BAi​​
1≤Bi≤M1 \leq B_i \leq M1≤Bi​≤M
Since the answer may be large, print it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT − the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains 222 lines of input:

The first line of each test case contains two space separated integers NNN, MMM.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer on a newline - answer modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
